methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
RobCo is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $60M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company operating in the robotics and manufacturing sector, including peer-stage and adjacent-stage companies such as 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($60M) as a proxy for post-money value and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A progression, implies a valuation of $120M. Peer-median analysis across the 6-company comparable set—spanning stages from Series B with valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B—yields a median valuation of $1.1B. This wide range reflects material heterogeneity in the peer set, particularly the inclusion of outliers like 1X Technologies at $10.0B alongside smaller players at $300M–$500M.

Triangulating both methods produces a low estimate of $102M, a median of $598M, and a high of $1.2B. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer medians support the upper range. Key uncertainties include RobCo's relative technological maturity versus peer benchmarks, near-term revenue trajectory, and the appropriate stage adjustment from Series A to Series B comparables. The wide peer dispersion ($300M–$10.0B) underscores that comparable-driven valuation in this sector carries material model risk absent more granular performance data.
